description: RIGHT JOIN makes left-side columns nullable even if declared NOT NULL

columns.name: name
columns.note: LEFT side of RIGHT JOIN: users.name is NOT NULL in schema but becomes nullable

name: basic_right_join
query_sql:
-- @name GetOrdersWithOptionalUsers
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.name, o.id, o.total FROM users u RIGHT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks u.name as nullable because it is on the LEFT side of a RIGHT JOIN
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks u.name as non-nullable based on schema definition
